title: Math teacher chat — phased build
description: Private math-lesson instructor on the client chat surface: guide students through lessons stored in the data layer. Phases from simplest deliverable to full implementation.

items.title: Phase 1 — Talking teacher
items.body: Brand agent with a math-teacher persona and one lesson written into its instructions. Works with what exists today; zero build.

items.title: Phase 2 — Lessons from storage
items.body: Lessons stored as entities (CMS); chat fetches the chosen lesson and teaches from it.

items.title: Phase 3 — Student memory
items.body: Data door: signed-in students save progress and resume where they left off; records scoped per user server-side.

items.title: Phase 4 — Structured lessons
items.body: Lesson files with steps, quizzes, pass rules, branching, per-step guardrails, resume position, outcome records, and a version field.

items.title: Phase 5 — Visual pages from JSON primitives
items.body: Progress board and quiz pages beside the chat, structured as JSON compositions of the existing view-renderer platform primitives (stack/row/list/text/input/actions) — no free HTML. Pages call the platform only via the kody.call bridge; permissions enforced server-side.

items.title: Phase 6 — Authoring by chat
items.body: Teacher creates and edits lessons by chatting; engine writes lesson files and pages by our rules.

items.title: Phase 7 — Brand component libraries (escape hatch)
items.body: Only when a real brand outgrows the platform primitives: let a business ship its own component library (custom elements JS/CSS in its repo, any design complexity). The same page JSON then places brand components next to platform primitives; data/actions still flow only through the platform API with the user's identity.
